title: Add A/B test feature flag with sticky assignment
description: Implements sticky A/B test assignment using a feature flag system with user-based bucketing.
diff:
diff --git a/features/ab_test.py b/features/ab_test.py
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..c1d2e3f
--- /dev/null
+++ b/features/ab_test.py
@@ -0,0 +1,22 @@
+from __future__ import annotations
+import hashlib
+
+_assignments: dict[str, str] = {}  # In-memory cache of assignments
+
+def get_variant(experiment: str, user_id: str, variants: list[str]) -> str:
+    cache_key = f"{experiment}:{user_id}"
+    
+    if cache_key in _assignments:
+        return _assignments[cache_key]
+    
+    # Deterministic bucketing via hash
+    bucket = int(hashlib.md5(cache_key.encode()).hexdigest(), 16) % len(variants)
+    variant = variants[bucket]
+    
+    # Race condition: concurrent requests for the same user can:
+    # 1. Both miss the cache
+    # 2. Both compute the same variant (deterministic, so ok)
+    # 3. But if variants list changes between requests, different variants assigned
+    _assignments[cache_key] = variant
+    return variant
comments: tara: The in-memory _assignments dict is per-process. In a multi-worker deployment, different workers have different caches. A user hitting worker A gets variant 'control', then hitting worker B might get variant 'treatment' if the variants list was updated between requests. Need persistent storage for assignments.
uri: Also, the dict is not thread-safe. concurrent.futures or asyncio workers can corrupt it.
author-vera: I'll use Redis for persistent assignment storage.
author: vera
